currentState: Trusted command pair inside damaged persistence. Chapter 14 keeps Cyndia as the left-hand relay and interception edge while Sylvanas handles the larger pressure, preserving the line without asking it to feel healed.
powerDynamic: Sylvanas still directs the mission and decides pace. Cyndia owns the relay handoff, left-flank threat response, and the practical execution that lets Sylvanas keep her attention on the bigger road.

stateHistory.currentState: Professional trust inside a clear command chain. Cyndia reports cleanly; Sylvanas listens, tests, confirms, and then acts.
Professional trust under live field pressure. The command chain stays clear, but chapter 2 proves it works just as well once the problem leaves the outpost.
Trusted field pair under escalating disaster. Chapter 3 proves Sylvanas relies on Cyndia not just for obedience but for the kind of ground truth she can act on quickly.
Chapter 11 turns the chapter-9 survivor pair into a live two-rider instrument. They do not repair the missing flank; they move with it and still keep making correct turns.

Battle-tested command pair carrying a ghost in the formation. Sylvanas trusts Cyndia to read ground, horses, and relay pressure without explanation; Cyndia answers with immediate execution and almost no wasted language.
Trusted two-ranger line inside a damaged three-woman formation. In chapter 13 Cyndia owns the exterior thread so completely that Sylvanas can risk the descent alone.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Cyndia salutes briefly and falls into matched stride rather than forcing Sylvanas to stand still for the briefing.
Sylvanas stops, questions, and physically checks the ward-light and stone herself, treating Cyndia's report as solid enough to verify rather than suspect enough to challenge.

In chapter 2 the relationship is almost all compressed motion: Sylvanas issues short orders, Cyndia executes them instantly, and the rhythm never breaks even while the operation changes from custody to ride to combat.

Chapter 3 keeps them in that same rhythm, but the tasks shift to investigation — Cyndia already crouched over signs by the time Sylvanas turns toward her, Sylvanas trusting the count enough to build the next move around it.
Cyndia handles horses, left lane, and roofline while Sylvanas keeps the center, showing how practiced the division of labor has become.
After Lyriel dies, Cyndia's body keeps checking the empty right while Sylvanas keeps her on the left and then on the south-west road.
Cyndia rides the useful edge while Sylvanas reads the whole road, the old left-right teamwork compressed into a single surviving scout and one commander who now notices every missing beat.
At the end, the pair turns north almost as one body: dispatch handed over, horses already moving, no need for repeated orders.
They move through the crossing as practiced asymmetry: Sylvanas in the bow and on the wall, Cyndia on the oars and the hold-fast work.
Cyndia stays below the window with the bow across both hands while Sylvanas goes inside, making trust visible through position rather than speech.
Once the line reforms, Cyndia takes the left again while Sylvanas shifts half a step forward-right of Jaina, preserving formation even as its emotional geometry changes.

Chapter 14 turns their rhythm into relay-and-intercept work: Cyndia handing forward the stone, sliding crosswise to catch the rider, and then falling back into the left position the instant Sylvanas has the next direction.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsCyndia: Still direct first-name command shorthand, with no wasted cushioning between order and execution.
nameUsage.cyndiaCallsSylvanas: Still compressed formal obedience, but so practiced it carries whole tactical states in a word or two.

history.event: Cyndia sends the runner to the exact road where Sylvanas can be found, showing prior knowledge of her habits and confidence in how to reach her.
Sylvanas explicitly validates Cyndia's choice to hold the scene, secure the evidence, and wait for senior authority.
Sylvanas trusts Cyndia to drop the ward-circle, ride south, maintain escort discipline, and take lethal shots under pressure without needing elaborate instruction.
In Millhaven, Cyndia becomes Sylvanas's strongest on-ground corroboration, supplying cattle evidence, body estimates, and wagon-rut interpretation that convert suspicion into action.
Cyndia helps hold the farmhouse and compound actions together by managing horses, left-flank pressure, and route notes while Sylvanas carries center command.
Lyriel's death forces Cyndia into the one-woman version of a two-ranger flank, a change Sylvanas immediately reads and keeps beside her.
At the split, Sylvanas takes Cyndia with her toward Hearthglen and Arthas, marking Cyndia as the surviving field half of the broken formation.
The southbound ride through Eastweald proves they can still function as a fast investigative pair even after the formation is cut down to two.
When the Caer Darrow relay lands, Cyndia reads the redirect before Sylvanas has to repeat it, confirming how complete their working shorthand has become.
Cyndia finds the east-face window and keeps the crossing quiet enough for Sylvanas to enter without lighting the watched landing.
She holds the exit and then takes Jaina back into the line without asking for explanation the chapter has not earned yet.
Her Hearthglen relay is the line that turns recovery into immediate southbound motion.
Cyndia passes Lor'themar's recall stone forward and holds position while Sylvanas refuses the order without slowing.
When the south-track rider bursts from the lane, Cyndia moves first and gives Sylvanas the breathing room to hear the report instead of fight for it.
Sylvanas halves the relay estimate and Cyndia accepts the harsher pace without objection, proving the command shorthand remains intact even under emotional strain around them.

trajectory: Trusted report chain -> field pair -> grief-hardened survivor pair -> shore breach and relay partnership -> chapter 14's left-flank and relay discipline inside a line that stays damaged and functional at once.
